name: Logitech G915 TKL Tenkeyless LIGHTSPEED Wireless Gaming Keyboard

specs.Switches: Low-profile GL mechanical (Linear, Clicky, or Tactile) — 2.7mm travel, 1.5mm actuation
specs.Battery: 40 hours with RGB on; up to 1000 hours with RGB off
specs.Macro Keys: 5 dedicated G keys + 3 M-key macro profiles

whatWorks: Low-profile mechanical switches are the G915 TKL's defining characteristic. At 22mm total keyboard height and 2.7mm key travel (versus 4mm on standard mechanical), the G915 sits flat enough on a desk that wrist rests become optional rather than necessary. Gamers who type for extended periods and prefer a flatter hand angle consistently report less wrist strain compared to standard-height keyboards — the ergonomic benefit of low-profile is real and underappreciated.

LIGHTSPEED wireless at 1ms polling rate is tournament-grade, meaning the keyboard's wireless connection introduces no perceptible input latency versus a wired keyboard. Paired with Bluetooth 5.1 for dual-connect, the G915 TKL can simultaneously maintain a LIGHTSPEED connection to a gaming PC and a Bluetooth connection to a second device — switching audio or control without any re-pairing process.

The TKL (tenkeyless) form factor removes the numpad from the right side, shifting the keyboard left and bringing your mouse hand closer to your body's centerline. This is a meaningful ergonomic and space improvement for PC gaming: your mousing arm sits at a more natural angle and you gain significant mouse movement range without changing desk size. The G915 TKL pairs this ergonomic advantage with low-profile key height for a doubly comfortable setup.

Battery performance is exceptional in both modes. 40 hours with full RGB enabled is already competitive with wired keyboards that need no battery. Turn off the RGB and the G915 TKL reaches an extraordinary 1,000 hours on a single charge — making it the wireless keyboard that most closely approximates the no-battery-worry convenience of a wired keyboard. For anyone who has been frustrated by wireless keyboards dying mid-session, this figure is transformative.
worthKnowing: Low-profile GL switches have 2.7mm total travel — about 35% less than standard MX switches at 4mm. Mechanical switch enthusiasts who are accustomed to deeper key travel may find the GL switches feel less satisfying to type on, particularly if transitioning from heavier or clicky full-travel switches. The switches are excellent for gaming and light typing but may not satisfy heavy typist preferences.

Five dedicated G macro keys on the left side of the keyboard are useful for MMO, strategy, and MOBA players who rely on complex multi-key macros. FPS gamers who never use macros may find the additional columns on the keyboard's left edge unnecessary, though they do not interfere with standard typing or gaming. G HUB software is required to program macro functions.

The aluminum top plate is excellent for durability and premium feel, but the keyboard does not include a wrist rest. For a low-profile keyboard at 22mm, a wrist rest is rarely needed — the angle is natural for most gamers — but buyers with specific wrist health requirements should note there is no bundled wrist support.

Dual-connect wireless means both LIGHTSPEED and Bluetooth connections can be active simultaneously for audio or notifications, but the keyboard only inputs through one connection at a time. Switching from LIGHTSPEED gaming mode to Bluetooth for a different device requires pressing the Bluetooth button on the keyboard — it is a quick action but not instantaneous.

commonComplaints.title: Low-profile feel is divisive
commonComplaints.body: Experienced mechanical keyboard users who typed on Cherry MX switches for years find the GL low-profile switches less satisfying tactilely. The shorter travel and lighter actuation are well-suited to gaming but the typing feedback is qualitatively different from full-height switches, which generates polarized opinions.

commonComplaints.title: G HUB required for full customization
commonComplaints.body: Like other Logitech G products, macro assignment, RGB profiles, and advanced wireless settings require G HUB. The software works well for most users but occasional stability issues during Windows updates frustrate a subset of buyers.

commonComplaints.title: Price versus SteelSeries Apex Pro TKL
commonComplaints.body: At $229 CAD, the G915 TKL is directly priced against the SteelSeries Apex Pro TKL Wireless at $279 CAD. Several buyers note the Apex Pro's adjustable actuation OmniPoint switches are a more innovative feature than the GL switches, though the G915's 1,000-hour battery without RGB is unmatched.

commonComplaints.title: Limited switch options per variant
commonComplaints.body: The G915 TKL is available in GL Linear, GL Clicky, and GL Tactile variants, but each is sold as a separate purchase. Buyers who want to try different switch types must purchase different keyboards — there is no switch testing option or modular switch design.

verdict: The Logitech G915 TKL is the best low-profile wireless gaming keyboard available, combining LIGHTSPEED wireless reliability, premium aluminum build, and an extraordinary 1,000-hour battery life in the ergonomic tenkeyless form factor that serious PC gamers prefer. The GL low-profile switches may not satisfy every mechanical switch taste, but for the gaming context they are designed for, they perform excellently — and the keyboard's package of features at $229 CAD remains unmatched in wireless TKL gaming keyboards.

realBuyers.quote: The 22mm profile is a revelation. My wrists are flat on the desk without a wrist rest for the first time with a mechanical keyboard. The ergonomic improvement for extended gaming sessions is real and significant.
I turned off the RGB when I got this and have not charged it in four months. Four months of daily use and still showing full battery. The 1,000-hour claim is legitimately accurate.
The LIGHTSPEED wireless on a keyboard feels no different from wired. I tested deliberately with competitive games and could not identify any latency difference. The engineering justifies the price.

Dual-connect works exactly as advertised. LIGHTSPEED to my gaming PC, Bluetooth to my MacBook. Pressing the Bluetooth button switches inputs instantly. One keyboard for both machines without any cables.

metaDescription: Logitech G915 TKL review: Low-profile wireless TKL keyboard with LIGHTSPEED, 1000-hour battery without RGB, and aluminum build. $229 CAD on Amazon.ca.
introText: The Logitech G915 TKL LIGHTSPEED Wireless Gaming Keyboard (ASIN B085RMD5TP) is available on Amazon.ca for $229 CAD and delivers the lowest-profile mechanical gaming keyboard with dual-connect LIGHTSPEED and Bluetooth wireless, aluminum build quality, and a 1,000-hour battery life without RGB that makes wireless charging a non-issue. The tenkeyless layout provides the extra mouse space that competitive PC gamers consistently prefer.
